inline_comments.message:
The task counting (currently only used in a trace) needs to be updated to count correctly: 


 .doOnEach(signal -> {if (signal.isOnNext()) taskCounter.inc();})

(and the same for the decrement)
Let's extract constant with this path.
Can we handle port with value of -1 (returned by URI.getPort when port is missing)? If not maybe let's keep it as null or (even better) Option.empty()?

Is it possible for this string to contain only userName, as in for `http://user@host.com/` it will contain "user" ? If so, it's worth checking it here (return Option.empty when size != 2), because fileServerData expects array with two elements.
Reorder methods so public ones are first and they are in an usage order.

Flux defer makes sense almost only when preparing the flux in a public API. So if this method were public we should change it to `return Flux.defer(()-> jsonObject ? createMsgs(...) : getMsgsFromArr(...));`. The whole point of using defer is to defer the execution of this code to the moment of subscription, thus making the stream compliant with laziness of rx.

In this case I believe we can just skip this Flux.defer as this private method is used in getMessagesFromJson in flatMap operator which (as any other such operator) already defers the subscription.
Maybe we could refactor this method so it returns Optional<Scheme> so there would be no need to handle exception here. If we insist on logging the message we could do that there, too.
BTW. this whole class is growing and has many private methods. Maybe we can split it into few package-scoped classes?

If we know that the Flux will always contain at most one element, we should probably return Mono. Reactor API provides some nice additional features for Mono and an easy way to convert it to Flux if necessary.
Why are we caching this? Is is to support retry? If so maybe we can move retry logic to collectFile method so it will retry only the thing which needs retry. Just a suggestion.

This class does lots of IO. Maybe we should use Schedulers.elastic() for IO operations (removing file, reading from DMaaP, etc.) so we won't block other schedulers? See https://projectreactor.io/docs/core/release/reference/#schedulers
Can't we use AtomicInteger for it?
Does this class live for the whole time of running DFC? If so, is this set somehow cleared? Otherwise we have a memory leak.
If we expect this list to be big, we can use one of generation methods described in https://projectreactor.io/docs/core/release/reference/#producing. Otherwise, it's ok.
Can't we get rid of these IDE-specific comments? Maybe it would be better to reconfigure IDE (Eclipse?) instead of adding them everywhere?
Is this class a component/service (aka. singleton)? If so I believe this won't be thread-safe.
In the future we shall probably use DCAE-SDK here. So temporarily we can leave this method as-is. Otherwise, we can improve a few things.
We can just "throw Exception" in test method, leaving this check for JUnit framework.
I believe `Californium-SR4` is used in spring 5.1.4.RELEASE.
The port is required by the current implementation. If that can be optional, it will be implemented separately.
Done, public are first (but the order is not changed).
The scheme is not optional. There are too much Optionals and conditions in this code already when exceptions should be used.
That is a possible future improvement.

Yes, that is known. There are several places in this component that should return a Mono instead of a Flux. 
That will be a later change (but then in all places where it can be).

The caching is for the retry. 
The intention is to just to retry this (file collection). 
It seems to work fine.

We will probably experient more with this when we will test more massive load. 
But what I want here is a typical thread pool behavior in the sense that if too many items are issued, they should get queued (until there are available execution resources). If the threads are cached or not does probably not matter in this application. 

This is the description of the paralell scheduler: 
"that hosts a fixed pool of single-threaded ExecutorService-base workers and is suited for parallel work."

It is by design. It will remember all files handled by this process. 
If that is wise can be debated. The XNF will report not only new files but all files collected during the last 24 hours. So for each new file, ~100 already reported files are also included.
Cool, but we only expect the size to be ~100 items.
I agree. That can be a later thing. Everybody must then to the same reconfiguration.

No, this class should be OK. 
But there might be other singletons that must be fixed left after this commit. For instance key store.
Yes, I heard discussion about it.
There is another commit in the pipe that deal with the pom file.

OK. Fine. So port is required & there is no meaningful default.

In that case is the URI somehow validated, so we are sure that URI contains all necessary information? If not maybe we should throw exception in @Value.Check annotated method in FileServerData. This way we won't propagate invalid port number = -1 and wait for Sftp or Ftps client to fail.

OK. Just keep in mind that parallel scheduler is "Optimized for fast Runnable executions". If you throw too many blocking operations to it, it can slow down your entire app. Generally speaking it's recommended not to block parallel scheduler.

We discussed this. It will be fixed as an own task.
Each supported FTP protocol has a well known port. 
I agree that it then should be an optional.
Yes, we will see. Probably this might change later.

We need to figure out a way to remove files that are "out of scope" from this set. Maybe we can use a HashMap with sourceName as key and a list of published files as value instead. Then we can remove any files in the list that are no longer included in the latest message after the message has been handled. That would leave us with a list with about a hundred files per node at the maximum.
Yes, in https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/78373/ we step spring to 5.1.4.RELEASE. So in this commit here we should remove the version for reactor-bom since we don't need to specify the version here.
Yes, or maybe a timestamp updated each access and then purge in regular intervals.
Please add "target/" to the path so the collected file ends up in a folder that is ignored by git.

Put back the 2018 copyright notice for Nokia
Add a second line with a 2018-2019 Nordix notice

is this file based on another file from Nokia?
should you have done a "git mv" to rename?
should Nordix be 2019 ??

This isn't you.

If Henrik DID write this code, because this file is based on code from him, then you need to put back the NOKIA copyright notice.

Did you use a "git rm" instead of "git mv" to rename a previous file to this one?

2019?
if based on previous code from last year, then 2018-2019.
If based on code that NOKIA wrote, then include their copyright as well.

Put back the 2018 copyright notice
Change Nordix to 2018-2019.

Put back the 2018 copyright notice
Change Nordix to say 2018-2019

is this a rename of a previous file, or based on previous code?
If so, then it should say 2018-2019
If not, then it should say 2019

is this new code or based on previous code?
If new code, then 2019
If based on previous code, the 2018-2019
For a while there was two very similar implementations where one was probably based on the other .. but by now all lines in it are probably changed.
